title: Oculometric neurological examination (one) appliance
abstract: The invention concerns observation, measurement and analysis of a person' s eye movements while viewing a screen, for purposes of evaluating brain health, eye coordination or level of ability of a person for various pursuits such as sports.
claims:
Claims WE CLAIM: 1. An oculometric neurological examination apparatus, comprising : a display console including a screen to present moving or stationary objects to be viewed by the subject, at least one illuminator directed toward at least one eye of the subject, and a camera directed toward a position a subject's eyes will assume when the subject is engaged with the apparatus, electronics connected to receive images from the camera and produce signals representing eye movements of the subject, and the camera of the console being at a fixed position and distance from the position to be assumed by a subject's eyes, such that the camera will be focused on the eyes of a subject to be examined. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the display console includes the camera and illuminator in a common housing, the housing being physically connected to the head with a band or strap . 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the display console includes the camera and the illuminator in a common housing, and the apparatus including a frame having at one end a head contact on which the subject's head can rest in a fixed position, and at an opposite end the display console. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the display console has a base adapted to sit on a flat surface at a known position and the console with the camera and illuminator is fixedly positioned relative to said flat surface such that the camera is at said fixed position and distance from the eyes of 

a subject to be examined. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the camera has a lens of the size and focal length to allow the apparatus to capture images from one or both eyes of a subject, spanning the entire anthropometric range of inter-pupillary distance for adult humans. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, including a chin or forehead rest holds that holds a subject's head still such that the subject's eyes are at said fixed position and distance relative to the camera. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein said distance is in the range of about 50 to 60 cm. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the electronics include processing means for receiving the signals representing eye movements, for analyzing data from the signals and for comparing the data to benchmark data indicating normal or specific abnormal conditions. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the processing means further includes a quality control feature to assure signal quality by comparing camera image frames collected to a preselected geometric model of a human eye to ensure that images collected by the camera conform to a minimum quality standard, the feature providing an alert to a user or to the subject if signals fall below the minimum quality standard, indicating the signal data are inaccurate. 10. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the processing means has means for operating a plurality of different tracking modes, including an initial mode using the full field of view of the camera, at a relatively low sampling rate, in 

order to locate features of the subject's head and eyes, and an operational tracking mode wherein the electronic means samples a sub-region of the camera field of view at a much higher sampling rate for measurement of eye-movement behavior. 11. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the electronic means includes means for comparing recorded oculometric data from camera images with corresponding data recorded from any of a plurality of different reference populations having different characteristics. 12. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the electronic means has means to perform a plurality of different standardized analyses on oculometric data collected. 13. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the electronics include comparison means for making self-referenced comparisons, wherein the signals can be compared corresponding measurements recorded previously from the same subject. 14. The apparatus of claim 1, further including a self- archiving means for saving a series of unprocessed images from the camera to a storage medium in the event that an unusual form of eye movement is detected in the subject. 15. An oculometric neurological examination apparatus, comprising : a display console including a screen to present moving or stationary objects to be viewed by the subject, at least one illuminator directed toward at least one eye of the subject, a camera directed toward a position a subject's eyes will assume when the subject is engaged with the apparatus, the camera being at a fixed position and distance from the position to be assumed by a subject's eyes such that the 

camera will be focused on the eyes of a subject to be examined, first electronic means for receiving images from the camera and produce signals representing eye position or movement, second electronic means for computing a plurality of quality metrics from the analyzed eye images or eye position or movement signals, third electronic means for performing a plurality of standardized oculomotor protocols, fourth electronic means for performing a plurality of standardized oculometric analyses on the data collected, fifth electronic means for generating a plurality of standardized oculometrics from the data and analyses, and sixth electronic means for comparing oculometrics to oculometrics from normative databases. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, including a chin or forehead rest that holds a subject's head still such that the subject's eyes are at said fixed position and distance relative to the camera. 17. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the display console includes the camera and the illuminator in a common housing, and are physically connected to the head with a band or strap. 18. An oculometric neurological examination apparatus, comprising : a frame having at one end a chin rest and a forehead rest for a subject to be examined, at an opposite end of the frame, a display console including a screen to present moving objects to be viewed by the subject, at least one illuminator directed toward at least one eye of the subject, a camera directed toward a position a 

subject's eyes will assume when the object is engaged on the chin est and forehead rest, and electronics to receive images from the camera and produce signals representing eye movements of the subject, the camera of the console being at a fixed position and distance from the position to be assumed by a subject's eyes, such that the camera will be focused on the eyes of a subject to be examined. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the said distance is in the range of about 50 to 60 cm. 20. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the electronics include means for receiving the signals representing eye movements, for analyzing data from the signals and for comparing the data to benchmark data indicating normal or specific abnormal conditions.
